notes:
After starting the game it will open a black screen (showing only the mouse) for a few seconds and then open a small error message on top of it that is just saying "fatal error". Clicking OK then closes everything and that's it.
Tried PROTON_NO_ESYNC, PROTON_NO_D3D11 and PROTON_USE_WINED3D (individually from each other) which didn't make any difference.
title: The Cinema Rosa

notes:
It's me again. I could get it to run using the launch option -onethread, while that causes some stuttering it essentially it works. I've had huge artifacts on many walls in combination with light sources, but could easily fix that by setting Shadows to low in the video settings of the game. 
But in the end it's unplayable, because the videos on the projector screens don't play. As there is at least one puzzle in the game that is based on several of these videos, you wouldn't be able to finish the game.
title: The Cinema Rosa
